analysis.problem: LGBTQ+ youth mental health is deteriorating due to lack of institutional support and affirmation in educational settings
analysis.root_cause: School systems are not adequately addressing the mental health needs of LGBTQ+ students, despite evidence showing that affirming environments significantly reduce suicide risk
leverage_points: School administrators and policymakers who control educational resources and curriculum
Educators and counselors who interact directly with students

proposed_actions.action: Implement mandatory LGBTQ+ inclusivity training for all educators
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Reduce reported suicide risk among LGBTQ+ students by 30% within 3 years

proposed_actions.action: Create school-based mental health support programs specifically for LGBTQ+ youth
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Increase access to mental health services for LGBTQ+ students by 50% within 2 years

analysis.generalization: This problem connects to broader issues of institutional discrimination, systemic underfunding of mental health services, and the failure of educational systems to address social equity and inclusion
